refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and the system to achieve and maintain energy efficiency performance such that measured energy_per_ton (energy_used / ton_moved) does not exceed the customer-defined E_target over the agreed evaluation period, under nominal operating conditions and across the range of supported worksite scenarios.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-003 as stated is a KPI support requirement but lacks specificity on measurement scope, boundary conditions, and operational context. Refinement clarifies that energy efficiency is a measurable outcome dependent on operator behavior, vehicle efficiency, and system-level optimization. The requirement now explicitly bounds the evaluation period and scenario scope, making it verifiable and traceable to design decisions.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Establish baseline energy consumption and ton-moved metrics during controlled field trials. Instrument the vehicle with energy sensors (fuel/battery consumption) and load/distance sensors. Collect data across representative worksite scenarios (terrain, load profiles, operator skill levels). Calculate energy_per_ton for each scenario and aggregate over the evaluation period. Compare against E_target with documented acceptance criteria (e.g., ≤ E_target with ±5% measurement uncertainty).
sub_frs: FR-3.1: The system shall measure and log energy consumption (fuel or battery) with ≤5% accuracy.
FR-3.2: The system shall measure and log productive work output (ton_moved) with ≤5% accuracy.
FR-3.3: The system shall provide real-time and historical energy efficiency feedback to operators to enable behavioral optimization.
FR-3.4: The system shall optimize vehicle powertrain and operational parameters to minimize energy consumption per unit work.
FR-3.5: The system shall ensure that teleoperation and safety services do not degrade energy efficiency below acceptable thresholds.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement onboard energy and work measurement subsystem; provide operator feedback via HMI; optimize powertrain via closed-loop control based on real-time efficiency metrics.
DP-B: Integrate energy monitoring into teleoperation system; centralize efficiency optimization logic in remote tele-operator station; push optimization commands to vehicle.

DP-C: Distribute energy measurement across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules; implement hybrid local/remote optimization with Safety Services veto authority; aggregate metrics at NGHE Solution level for reporting.
DP-D: Implement energy efficiency as a constraint in task scheduling; prioritize low-energy task sequences; monitor and alert when efficiency drifts below E_target.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Distribute energy measurement across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules; implement hybrid local/remote optimization with Safety Services veto authority; aggregate metrics at NGHE Solution level for reporting.

potential_sources: Energy measurement subsystem (Vehicle) must interface cleanly with Telemetry interface to avoid tight coupling on data format.
Optimization logic split between Vehicle (local control) and Teleoperation (remote advisory) requires clear handoff protocol via Tasking interface.
Safety Services veto on optimization commands (e.g., disable aggressive acceleration) must not create circular dependencies; Safety Services should be read-only observer of efficiency metrics.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C avoids over-centralization (DP-B) and over-distribution (DP-A). It respects the three-module architecture and allows Safety Services to enforce constraints without owning optimization logic. Coupling is manageable via well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert).

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle powertrain supports closed-loop efficiency control (e.g., engine load management, transmission optimization). Risk: Legacy or third-party powertrains may not expose sufficient control parameters.

Assumption: Teleoperation latency is low enough (<500 ms) that remote optimization commands remain effective. Risk: High-latency networks may make remote optimization infeasible; fallback to local-only control required.
Assumption: Energy and work measurement sensors are available and calibrated. Risk: Sensor drift or miscalibration could invalidate efficiency metrics and acceptance criteria.

Assumption: Safety Services can be integrated as a non-blocking observer. Risk: If Safety Services must actively control powertrain (e.g., emergency shutdown), it may conflict with efficiency optimization.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the authority model: Vehicle owns local efficiency optimization (primary); Teleoperation provides advisory optimization (secondary); Safety Services has veto authority only for safety-critical actions (e.g., speed limiting, emergency stop). Document the fallback behavior if Teleoperation link is lost (revert to local-only optimization).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level energy efficiency orchestration, measurement aggregation, reporting, and acceptance criteria validation. Owns the energy_per_ton KPI calculation and historical trend analysis. Coordinates between Vehicle (local measurement and control), Teleoperation (remote optimization advisory), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives energy consumption (kWh or liters/hour) and work output (ton, distance) from Vehicle. Frequency: 1 Hz or event-driven. Format: structured data with timestamp, sensor ID, and confidence metrics.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends optimization commands (e.g., target efficiency mode, power limit) to Vehicle and receives acknowledgment. Receives advisory optimization suggestions from Teleoperation and forwards to Vehicle if Safety Services approves.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraints and veto signals from Safety Services (e.g., 'disable aggressive acceleration', 'limit speed to 10 km/h'). NGHE Solution applies these constraints to optimization logic before forwarding to Vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity to Teleoperation module for remote optimization advisory and to external reporting systems for KPI submission. Handles graceful degradation if link is lost.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of system-level KPI orchestration, measurement aggregation, and acceptance criteria validation. Energy efficiency is a system-level property that depends on Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Containment is high because the KPI calculation and reporting logic is centralized here. Diffusion is minimal (only interface definitions leak to other modules).

Vehicle owns local energy measurement and powertrain control. However, it cannot own the system-level KPI because it lacks visibility into Teleoperation optimization and Safety Services constraints. If energy efficiency logic were entirely in Vehicle, it would be tightly coupled to Teleoperation and Safety Services, violating separation of concerns. Diffusion is moderate: Vehicle must expose energy/work measurement via Telemetry and accept optimization commands via Tasking.

Teleoperation can provide remote optimization advisory (e.g., suggest lower-energy task sequences), but it should not own the KPI or central optimization logic. Doing so would create tight coupling between remote operations and system-level efficiency, making the system fragile if Teleoperation link is lost. Diffusion is high: optimization logic would be split between Teleoperation and Vehicle, creating maintenance burden.

Safety Services should not own energy efficiency KPI or optimization logic. Its role is to enforce safety constraints (e.g., speed limits, emergency stops) that may impact efficiency. If Safety Services owned efficiency, it would conflate safety and efficiency concerns, violating single responsibility principle. Diffusion is high: safety constraints would be scattered across multiple modules.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the optimal owner because: (1) it has system-level visibility into all three contributing modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services); (2) the energy_per_ton KPI is a system-level property, not a local concern; (3) measurement aggregation and reporting are naturally centralized; (4) it can coordinate between local and remote optimization without creating circular dependencies.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution becomes a 'god module' if it also owns other system-level concerns (e.g., task scheduling, fleet management). Mitigation: Clearly scope NGHE Solution to energy efficiency KPI only; delegate other concerns to appropriate modules.

Risk: If Teleoperation link is lost, NGHE Solution cannot receive remote optimization advice. Mitigation: Design fallback to local-only optimization in Vehicle; NGHE Solution monitors link status and alerts operator.

Risk: Safety Services constraints may be applied too late (after optimization commands are sent to Vehicle). Mitigation: NGHE Solution applies Safety Services constraints before forwarding Tasking commands; implement constraint validation logic in NGHE Solution.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Explicitly define the data flow: (1) Vehicle measures energy and work, sends via Telemetry to NGHE Solution; (2) NGHE Solution calculates energy_per_ton and compares to E_target; (3) NGHE Solution requests Safety Services approval for optimization; (4) NGHE Solution forwards approved optimization to Vehicle via Tasking; (5) Teleoperation provides advisory optimization via Network Link, which NGHE Solution evaluates and forwards if approved. Document the fallback behavior if any link is lost.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid local/remote optimization with Safety Services veto) directly addresses the refined FR (achieve energy_per_ton ≤ E_target). The DP specifies a clear mechanism: measure energy and work, optimize locally and remotely, apply safety constraints, and aggregate metrics. The DP is implementable and testable. No coupling issues identified that would prevent realization.

diffusion_risks: Moderate risk: Energy measurement logic is distributed across Vehicle (sensor interface) and NGHE Solution (aggregation and KPI calculation). Mitigation: Define clear Telemetry interface contract to minimize coupling.

Moderate risk: Optimization logic is split between Vehicle (local control) and Teleoperation (remote advisory). Mitigation: Define clear Tasking interface and handoff protocol; document fallback if Teleoperation link is lost.

Low risk: Safety Services veto authority could create circular dependencies if not carefully designed. Mitigation: Safety Services is read-only observer; NGHE Solution applies constraints before forwarding commands.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the clear owner of system-level KPI orchestration. Vehicle and Teleoperation are contributors (measurement and advisory optimization). Safety Services is a constraint enforcer. Interfaces are well-defined (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). No module is overloaded; responsibilities are clear.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. Energy and work measurement can be instrumented on Vehicle. KPI calculation is straightforward (energy_per_ton = energy_used / ton_moved). Acceptance criteria can be validated via field trials over the agreed evaluation period. Telemetry and Tasking interfaces are observable and testable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear. NGHE Solution owns KPI calculation and reporting. Vehicle owns measurement and local control. Teleoperation owns remote advisory. Safety Services owns constraint enforcement. Each module has a well-defined role and interface. Traceability from FR to DP to IM is explicit.

recommended_revisions: Refine the acceptance criteria: specify the evaluation period (e.g., 30 days of field operation), the range of worksite scenarios (e.g., terrain types, load profiles), and the measurement uncertainty tolerance (e.g., ±5%).
Document the fallback behavior if Teleoperation link is lost: Vehicle reverts to local-only optimization; NGHE Solution alerts operator and continues KPI monitoring.

Define the Safety Services constraint model: list the constraints that Safety Services can impose (e.g., speed limits, power limits) and the mechanism for applying them (e.g., NGHE Solution queries Safety Services before forwarding optimization commands).

Specify the optimization algorithm: is it rule-based (e.g., 'reduce engine load if efficiency is below threshold'), model-predictive (e.g., 'predict future efficiency and adjust proactively'), or learning-based (e.g., 'learn operator behavior and suggest optimizations')? This will inform the complexity of the DP and IM.

decision.justification: The refined FR is well-scoped and verifiable. The selected DP (hybrid local/remote optimization with Safety Services veto) is sound and addresses the FR without excessive coupling. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution as orchestrator) is appropriate and has high containment. However, the FR is not atomic; it decomposes into five sub-FRs (measurement, feedback, optimization, safety integration). Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to define the detailed design parameters and implementation modules. Proceeding to the next level will clarify the measurement subsystem, optimization algorithms, and interface contracts.
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR explicitly lists five sub-FRs: (1) energy measurement, (2) work output measurement, (3) operator feedback, (4) powertrain optimization, (5) safety integration. Each sub-FR is a candidate for the next decomposition level. The gate review confirms that the current level is sound, but further decomposition is necessary to reach atomic, implementable requirements.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-3.1: The system shall measure and log energy consumption (fuel or battery) with ≤5% accuracy.
FR-3.2: The system shall measure and log productive work output (ton_moved) with ≤5% accuracy.
FR-3.3: The system shall provide real-time and historical energy efficiency feedback to operators to enable behavioral optimization.
FR-3.4: The system shall optimize vehicle powertrain and operational parameters to minimize energy consumption per unit work.
FR-3.5: The system shall ensure that teleoperation and safety services do not degrade energy efficiency below acceptable thresholds.

assumptions: Vehicle powertrain supports closed-loop efficiency control (e.g., engine load management, transmission optimization).
Teleoperation latency is low enough (<500 ms) that remote optimization commands remain effective.
Energy and work measurement sensors are available and calibrated with ≤5% accuracy.
Safety Services can be integrated as a non-blocking observer without creating circular dependencies.
The evaluation period and worksite scenarios are defined by the customer and are stable over the project lifecycle.
Operators are willing to adopt efficiency feedback and optimization recommendations.
open_risks: Sensor drift or miscalibration could invalidate efficiency metrics and acceptance criteria. Mitigation: Implement sensor health monitoring and periodic recalibration.
High-latency networks may make remote optimization infeasible. Mitigation: Design fallback to local-only optimization; document performance degradation.

Safety Services constraints may conflict with efficiency optimization (e.g., emergency stop reduces efficiency). Mitigation: Clearly define the priority order (safety > efficiency); document trade-offs.
Operator behavior may not align with optimization recommendations, limiting the effectiveness of the system. Mitigation: Implement operator training and incentive mechanisms.
evidence_needed: Baseline energy consumption and ton-moved metrics from field trials (at least 30 days of operation across representative worksite scenarios).
Sensor specifications and calibration certificates for energy and work measurement devices.
Teleoperation network latency measurements under various conditions (good signal, poor signal, link loss).
Safety Services constraint model and priority order (documented in safety plan).
Operator feedback on the usability and effectiveness of energy efficiency feedback and optimization recommendations.
